mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and lower corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term assets combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates expanding liquidity and consumer purchasing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening economic momentum and higher near-term corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration correlates with negative 5-day returns during expansion phases, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 50 percent across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns across multiple market cycles, with Rank IC consistently near zero over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 245.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in greater than 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL crosses above 245.0, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside a declining unemployment rate indicate synchronized strength in residential investment and labor demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when HOUST rises but UNRATE remains flat, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing with t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeds 2.0 percent, confirming robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the signal reverses direction during periods of elevated VIX readings above 20.
